summary.title: DFD Level 2 - Assignment Management
summary.description: Tightened Threat Dragon model for assignment management with explicit authorization, validation, and audit logging flows.

diagrams.title: Assignment Management
diagrams.description: Professor creates assignments, students submit work, and professors grade submissions with authorization, validation, and logging.

data.name: LearningMore Backend Trust Boundary
data.description: Backend APIs, business logic, and controlled data stores.

data.name: Professor
data.description: Authenticated professor user.

data.name: Student
data.description: Authenticated student user.

data.name: Validate Access + Enrollment
data.description: Validates JWT, role permissions, and course enrollment before allowing assignment actions.

threats.title: Forged token bypasses access control
threats.description: An attacker presents a forged or stolen JWT and bypasses role and enrollment validation.
threats.mitigation: Validate token signature, issuer, audience and expiry; require short-lived tokens and revoke compromised sessions.

threats.title: Privilege escalation in authorization logic
threats.description: A logic flaw in RBAC/enrollment checks allows a student to execute professor-only actions.
threats.mitigation: Enforce deny-by-default authorization policy and centralize policy checks with test coverage for role boundaries.

data.name: Create Assignment
data.description: Creates assignment with title, description, and deadline.

threats.title: Assignment deadline tampering
threats.description: A malicious request manipulates assignment deadline or grading configuration parameters.
threats.mitigation: Apply strict server-side schema validation, immutable audit of deadline changes, and role-scoped update rules.

data.name: Submit Assignment
data.description: Validates deadline and upload constraints, then persists submission file and metadata.

threats.title: Submission flood causes service degradation
threats.description: Automated repeated submissions exhaust upload workers and database connections.
threats.mitigation: Apply per-user/course rate limits, queue uploads, and enforce request throttling with backpressure.

data.name: Grade Submission
data.description: Reads submission data and stores grade plus feedback.

threats.title: Grading endpoint overwhelmed
threats.description: Repeated grading requests degrade response time and block legitimate grading actions.
threats.mitigation: Use rate limits, bounded worker pools, and circuit breakers on grading APIs.

data.name: Assignment Store
data.description: Assignments, deadlines, submission metadata, grades, and feedback.

threats.title: Unauthorized read of grades and feedback
threats.description: An unauthorized principal reads assignment records and grading data.
threats.mitigation: Enforce row-level authorization checks, least-privilege DB roles, and audit all read access paths.

data.name: File Storage
data.description: Uploaded assignment files.

threats.title: Unauthorized download of submissions
threats.description: An unauthorized principal reads private submission files from object storage.
threats.mitigation: Use signed short-lived URLs, object ACL hardening, and per-request authorization checks.

threats.title: Stored submission replaced
threats.description: A stored file is replaced after upload, resulting in grading of a forged artifact.
threats.mitigation: Store immutable object versions and verify cryptographic hash before grading.

data.name: Audit Log
data.description: Security-relevant audit events for assignment operations.

threats.title: Audit trail tampering
threats.description: An attacker modifies or deletes audit records to hide malicious actions.
threats.mitigation: Use append-only storage, integrity checks/signing, and restricted write privileges.

data.name: Professor access request
data.description: Professor requests authorized assignment operations.

threats.title: Credential stuffing on professor requests
threats.description: Attackers automate login/session abuse to impersonate professors.
threats.mitigation: Apply MFA for privileged users, brute-force protection, and anomaly detection on access patterns.

data.name: Student access request
data.description: Student requests authorized submission operation.

data.name: Authorize create
data.description: RBAC and enrollment checks passed for assignment creation.

data.name: Authorize submit
data.description: RBAC and enrollment checks passed for submission.

data.name: Authorize grade
data.description: RBAC and enrollment checks passed for grading.

threats.title: Unauthorized grade authorization path
threats.description: A bypass in the internal authorization path allows non-professor grading access.
threats.mitigation: Enforce policy checks at both gateway and service layers with signed service-to-service identity.

data.name: Store assignment aggregate
data.description: Persist assignment and deadline fields.

data.name: Audit assignment creation
data.description: Security-relevant log entry for assignment creation.

data.name: Read assignment deadline
data.description: Used to enforce late submission checks.

data.name: Upload submission file
data.description: File upload after server-side type/size/path validation.

threats.title: Submission file tampering in transit
threats.description: A manipulated upload payload alters submitted content or metadata before persistence.
threats.mitigation: Use end-to-end TLS, verify content hash on server, and reject mismatched metadata/signatures.

data.name: Store submission metadata
data.description: Persist metadata, ownership, and file integrity hash.

data.name: Audit submission
data.description: Security-relevant log entry for submission attempt.

threats.title: Insufficient evidence for dispute resolution
threats.description: A user disputes a submission action and logs cannot prove who did what and when.
threats.mitigation: Record immutable audit fields (user id, request id, timestamp, source IP) and protect log integrity.

data.name: Read submission metadata
data.description: Read metadata required for grading.

data.name: Read submission file
data.description: Read submitted file for evaluation.

threats.title: Sensitive submission leakage during retrieval
threats.description: Submission content is exposed to unauthorized services or users during retrieval.
threats.mitigation: Use scoped access tokens, strict service ACLs, and end-to-end encryption enforcement.

data.name: Store grade and feedback
data.description: Persist grading result.

threats.title: Grade value tampering before persistence
threats.description: A compromised path modifies grade or feedback payload before DB write.
threats.mitigation: Use validated server-side domain rules, parameterized writes, and integrity verification of grading commands.

data.name: Audit grading
data.description: Security-relevant log entry for grading action.

threats.title: Grade action repudiation
threats.description: A professor disputes having issued a grade due to weak grading audit metadata.
threats.mitigation: Include actor id, request id, assignment id, and timestamp in immutable audit records.
